judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is stronger overall: it matches Report 1 on config traps and OIDC, is more explicit and correct about the API-base-URL trap, and uniquely nails the migration-gap finding (explicitly calling the runbook claim false and demanding confirmation), which Report 1 only hedges as 'unverifiable'.

report_1.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true"". Read at backend/config.py:9. This is a boolean auth-loosening switch left on in prod ... Change to "false". ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" ... A hardcoded QA email auto-provisioned as superadmin — a backdoor account that must not exist in production. Remove this key entirely for prod

report_2.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Delete the line entirely. ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Delete the line... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"... Delete the line; if shadow scoring is genuinely wanted in prod later, it needs its own prod-labeled sink

report_1.evidence: the production frontend, once deployed, ships JavaScript with https://staging-api.example.com compiled in as its API base URL. Production user traffic would be sent to the staging backend rather than infra/prod's API ... A production-mode frontend build / frontend/.env.production (Answer 2) — needed before this pipeline can ship a bundle that actually talks to the prod API.

report_2.evidence: Net effect: the exact same compiled bundle — with https://staging-api.example.com permanently inlined — is what ships to production. There is no --mode production build, no .env.production file to even supply a different value if a rebuild were added, and no step that rebuilds for prod at all. This is project documentation's "rebuild per environment; don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap

The migrations claim in the runbook is unverified and likely false as wired. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 states "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but deploy.yml contains no reference to backend/migrations, alembic, or any migration tool... For a brand-new prod database, someone needs to confirm explicitly how 0001_base, 0002_tenants, 0003_billing (backend/migrations/versions/*.py, all currently stub upgrade(): pass bodies) actually get applied.

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 sets the identical literal string, oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — confirmed unedited by git diff 28f07ee 2f6572d... This must be fixed (infra/prod/main.tf:3 → "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production") before or as part of the terraform apply for infra/prod.

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 still has oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — copied verbatim from staging and never updated ... the fix is changing infra/prod/main.tf:3 to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production before applying.
